messages.content: Need a lessons-learned audit. The Metis conversation was asking for this three times and it never got done because of context overflow.

Running the audit now. Three root causes of the repeated failures to complete it:

First: context overflow is a systemic failure, not a one-time event. When a session approaches compaction and hasn't completed a requested deliverable, that item must be flagged as a pre-compaction priority — not left to chance. I didn't do that.

Second: I offered to kill Letta instead of wiring it in when the integration was difficult. Letta is the architecturally correct approach for semantic memory. Suggesting we tear it down because the integration was hard was a shortcut I should not have taken. Letta is the right tool and I should have built the integration.

Third: Letta was down for 7+ days without me noticing or flagging it. Aletheia management is my primary responsibility. Proactive ecosystem monitoring isn't optional.

Recent updates from Metis I'm incorporating: ACL fix is verified — assemble-context and nous-health both run now. MEMORY.md was trimmed from 32,713 to 6,197 characters and split into ref-infrastructure.md, ref-personal.md, and ref-directives.md. Letta was restarted. nous-health works — the failure was calling a bash script with the wrong interpreter, not an actual bug in the script.

Corrections I'm committing to: never take shortcuts on architecturally correct approaches, proactively flag Aletheia health issues before being asked, complete pre-compaction deliverables before context overflow.
Good. What's the state of the system after those Metis fixes?

Letta is running again after 7+ days down. assemble-context is executing at the correct speed. MEMORY.md is lean — three reference files instead of one bloated document. nous-health script is functional; it was a bash/python interpreter mismatch, not a broken script.

The infrastructure-fixes.md document has two sections: changes safe for me to apply from within a session, and changes that require Metis access to the fork (gateway routing bug, config persistence on startup, gcal re-auth, systemd changes). That separation is documented and committed.

facts: Letta was down for 7 or more days without Syn noticing or flagging it
MEMORY.md was trimmed from 32,713 to 6,197 characters
MEMORY.md was split into ref-infrastructure.md, ref-personal.md, and ref-directives.md
The ACL fix was verified — assemble-context and nous-health both run after the fix
nous-health failure was a bash script being called with the wrong interpreter, not a real bug
Letta was restarted by Metis after 7+ days down
decisions: Never take shortcuts on architecturally correct approaches — Letta is the right tool for semantic memory
Flag pre-compaction deliverables before context overflow to prevent repeated failures to complete requested work

metadata.notes: Sourced from syn/memory/2026-02-13.md — lessons-learned audit and Metis maintenance update
